message:
feat(core): dual-runtime compatibility surface for tokio→asupersync migration

Add the `runtime_compat` module providing a temporary abstraction layer
that lets callers use a unified API regardless of which async runtime
backs the application. This is the scaffolding for a gradual migration
from tokio to asupersync without requiring a flag-day switchover.

Provides feature-gated type aliases and wrappers for:
- Sync primitives: `Mutex<T>`, `RwLock<T>`, `Semaphore` with Deref-based
  ergonomics that forward to tokio or asupersync depending on the
  `asupersync-runtime` feature flag
- Channel modules: `mpsc` (bounded/unbounded) and `watch` (single-value
  broadcast) with unified Sender/Receiver types
- Runtime lifecycle: `RuntimeBuilder` (current_thread / multi_thread),
  `CompatRuntime` wrapping block_on/spawn, and `Runtime` type alias
- Time helpers: `sleep()` and `timeout()` that delegate to the active
  runtime's timer implementation

Without the `asupersync-runtime` feature, all types transparently alias
to their tokio equivalents, ensuring zero overhead for the default build.

Includes smoke tests verifying runtime construction, future execution,
mpsc/watch channels, sleep/timeout behavior, semaphore acquisition,
and Mutex/RwLock contention across both runtime backends.

This module is explicitly temporary — it will be removed once the
asupersync migration is complete and all callers have been updated
to use asupersync primitives directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
